mission.title: Install and Configure Weaviate
mission.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database that combines vector search with structured filtering, making it ideal for applications like semantic search and recommendation engines. It offers cloud-native scalability and fault tolerance, but requires careful configuration for optimal performance and resource management.

steps.title: Install the Weaviate CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Weaviate command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Weaviate deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/releases/download/v1.36.0/weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/weaviate

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/releases/download/v1.36.0/weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/weaviate

steps.title: Create a Weaviate cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Weaviate cluster on your managed Kubernetes service with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create weaviate-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Weaviate cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials weaviate-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials and updates your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your Weaviate cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Weaviate using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Weaviate on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, which simplifies deployment and management.

```bash

helm repo add semitechnologies https://charts.semitechnologies.com

helm install weaviate semitechnologies/weaviate --version 1.36.0 --namespace weaviate --create-namespace

```

> Add the Weaviate Helm chart repository.
> This command installs Weaviate in the 'weaviate' namespace.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Weaviate deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment weaviate --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace weaviate

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> This command sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

steps.title: Cost warning
steps.description:
Be aware that running a managed Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

```bash

echo 'Monitor your Google Cloud billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

```

> This is a reminder to keep track of your cloud expenses.
commands: echo 'Monitor your Google Cloud billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Weaviate
uninstall.description:
This step removes Weaviate from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are made.

```bash

helm uninstall weaviate --namespace weaviate

gcloud container clusters delete weaviate-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command removes the Weaviate deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Weaviate
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your data. This process includes draining the nodes, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade weaviate semitechnologies/weaviate --version 1.36.0 --namespace weaviate

kubectl get pods --namespace weaviate

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name to cordon and drain it.
> This command upgrades Weaviate to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version may not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image version in your Helm chart and ensure it matches the available versions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Review the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace weaviate' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated to meet the Weaviate requirements.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check your service configuration and ensure it is set to type LoadBalancer or NodePort.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vectorization models and improved RBAC features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old vectorization API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.35.0, update your API calls to use the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods --namespace weaviate' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/releases/download/v1.36.0/weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/weaviate

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
